content_en:
As persecution intensified in Mecca and no prospect of relief appeared on the Arabian horizon, the Prophet ﷺ directed a group of his Companions to seek refuge in the Christian kingdom of Abyssinia (al-Habasha) — the realm of the Negus (al-Najashi), King Ashama ibn Abjar, whom the Prophet ﷺ described as a just king in whose land no one was wronged. This migration — the first Hijra in Islamic history — represents a pivotal moment: for the first time, the nascent Muslim community reached beyond the Arabian Peninsula to seek recognition and sanctuary from a non-Muslim ruler.

The first migration, in the fifth year of prophethood, consisted of approximately eleven men and four women. Among them were Uthman ibn Affan and his wife Ruqayyah (the Prophet's ﷺ daughter), Zubayr ibn al-Awwam, Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf, Abu Salamah and his wife Umm Salamah (who would later become one of the Prophet's ﷺ wives), and Ja'far ibn Abi Talib — the Prophet's ﷺ cousin who would become the spokesman for the Muslim delegation.

The Quraysh sent two of their most capable diplomats — Amr ibn al-As and Abdullah ibn Abi Rabia — with valuable gifts for the Negus and his courtiers, instructing them to demand the return of the Muslim emigrants as apostates from the ancestral religion. The Negus, however, insisted on hearing the Muslims speak for themselves before making any judgment. Ja'far ibn Abi Talib then delivered one of the most celebrated speeches in early Islamic history. He described the pre-Islamic condition of the Arabs — their idol worship, their moral degradation, the exploitation of the weak — and the transformation that Islam had brought. He described the Muslim beliefs regarding Allah, prophethood, the Day of Judgment, and Jesus (Isa ibn Maryam).

When the Negus asked about their view of Jesus, Ja'far recited verses from Surah Maryam — the Quranic account of the miraculous birth of Jesus and the mission of John the Baptist. The Negus wept until his beard was wet, and his bishops wept as well. The Negus then drew a line on the ground with his staff and said: 'By Allah, the difference between what we believe about Jesus and what you say is not greater than this line.' He refused to hand over the Muslims and returned the Qurayshi gifts, declaring: 'No, by God, I will not hand them over even if you gave me a mountain of gold.'

A second, larger migration followed shortly after — approximately eighty-three men and nineteen women — when false news reached Abyssinia that the Meccans had accepted Islam, prompting many to return home, only to find the persecution had intensified. The Negus eventually accepted Islam when the Prophet's ﷺ letter of invitation reached him in Medina years later. When the Negus died, the Prophet ﷺ performed the funeral prayer for him in absentia (salat al-gha'ib) — the only such prayer recorded in the Sunnah for a non-present deceased — honoring the man who had sheltered his community in their darkest hours.
content_id:
Ketika penganiayaan semakin parah di Makkah dan tidak ada prospek keringanan yang terlihat di cakrawala Arab, Nabi ﷺ mengarahkan sekelompok sahabatnya untuk mencari perlindungan di kerajaan Kristen Abyssinia (Al-Habasyah) — kerajaan An-Najasyi (Raja Ashama bin Abjar), yang Nabi ﷺ gambarkan sebagai raja yang adil yang tidak ada seorang pun yang dizalimi di negerinya.

Hijrah pertama ke Abyssinia terdiri dari 15 orang (11 laki-laki dan 4 perempuan). Kemudian hijrah kedua yang lebih besar menyusul, terdiri dari 83 laki-laki dan beberapa wanita. Di antara mereka adalah Utsman bin Affan (ra) dan istrinya Ruqayyah binti Muhammad (ra) — putri Nabi ﷺ.

Qurasiy mengirim utusan kepada An-Najasyi — Amr bin Al-Ash dan Abdullah bin Abi Rabi'ah — dengan hadiah-hadiah berharga untuk meminta An-Najasyi mengembalikan para pengungsi Muslim. An-Najasyi memanggil kaum Muslim dan meminta penjelasan tentang agama mereka. Jafar bin Abi Thalib (ra) mewakili mereka dan membacakan ayat-ayat dari Surah Maryam tentang Maryam dan Isa (as). An-Najasyi menangis dan berkata: "Wallahi, tidak ada perbedaan antara apa yang kamu katakan tentang Isa dan kebenaran sejauh lebar seutas benang ini." Ia menolak permintaan Quraisy dan memberikan perlindungan penuh kepada kaum Muslim.

Hijrah ke Abyssinia mengajarkan bahwa seorang Muslim boleh mencari perlindungan dari penganiayaan bahkan di negara yang diperintah oleh non-Muslim yang adil. Keadilan adalah nilai universal yang Islam akui di mana pun ia ditemukan.
content_ar:
حين اشتد الأذى في مكة ولاح أن لا أفق للفرج في الجزيرة، وجّه النبي ﷺ فريقاً من أصحابه للالتجاء إلى مملكة الحبشة المسيحية — بلد النجاشي الملك أصحمة بن أبجر، الذي وصفه النبي ﷺ بأنه ملك لا يُظلم عنده أحد.

وكانت الهجرة على دفعتين: الأولى نحو خمسة عشر رجلاً في السنة الخامسة، ثم موجة أكبر حين تبيّن أن الأذى لم ينقطع. وبلغت المجموعة الثانية نحو ثمانية وثمانين رجلاً وامرأة. وكان عثمان بن عفان وزوجته رقية بنت النبي ﷺ من الرحلتين معاً.

واتجه زعماء قريش إلى الحبشة بعثةً تحمل هدايا لاسترداد اللاجئين، فاستمع النجاشي إلى الطرفين. وتلا جعفر بن أبي طالب رضي الله عنه آيات من سورة مريم فأدهشت النجاشيَّ وأبكته، وأعلن أنه لن يُسلّم القوم ما داموا في حمايته. وهذه الحادثة تُعدّ من أولى نجاحات الدبلوماسية الإسلامية.
